figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of the Zero-Run Privacy Auditing framework. Observational data (Xi) from members and non-members introduces a confounding distribution shift (P1 ̸= P0). By modeling this shift, a propensity score estimator π̂(Xi) adjusts raw membership guesses (Ti) via global composition or pointwise conditioning, isolating true algorithmic leakage to yield valid empirical DP lower bounds.

Figure 2: Evaluation of zero-run auditing under both controlled synthetic conditions and real-world distribution shifts. (a) Privacy lower bounds for different distribution shifts; (b) distribution and lower quantile of the boostrapped privacy lower bounds.; (c) Propensity score distribution on iWildCam.
